nodes.label: MioBridgeCore Facade
nodes.rationale: @miobridge/core composes artifact generation and status through the explicit MioBridgeCore facade; runtime paths, state, kernels, metadata, clock, and source collectors are injected without frontend imports or module singletons.

nodes.label: @miobridge/core Workspace Package
nodes.rationale: Framework-independent backend services live in the private @miobridge/core package so composition has no frontend imports or module singletons.

nodes.label: frontend/src/server/core.ts Node Composition Adapter
nodes.rationale: Node-only composition adapter for @miobridge/core; API routes and SSR consume its MioBridgeCore/node aggregation instances while deployment and SSH lifecycle behavior remains frontend-owned.

nodes.label: StateStore Abstraction
nodes.rationale: Function instances share no filesystem on Vercel, so cluster state goes through StateStore: file backend under ~/.config/miobridge when self-hosted, Upstash/Vercel-KV Redis REST backend when env vars are set (required on Vercel).

nodes.label: CLI Dashboard Provider Boundary
nodes.rationale: packages/cli consumes public @miobridge/core exports headlessly; its versioned dashboard provider manifest and user-systemd launcher own only optional dashboard lifecycle, so provider removal preserves core config/data and lets fn-4 replace the artifact without changing CLI commands.

nodes.label: createRuntimePaths
nodes.rationale: Core runtime paths are resolved per instance through createRuntimePaths; frontend adapters must pass platform policies such as Vercel /tmp explicitly.

nodes.label: MIOBRIDGE_CONFIG_DIR Override
nodes.rationale: MIOBRIDGE_CONFIG_DIR can override the runtime base dir for isolated tests; on Vercel without it, runtime scratch/log paths fall back to /tmp/miobridge to avoid read-only home directory failures.

nodes.label: SSH Private Key StateStore Pattern
nodes.rationale: Uploaded SSH private keys use StateStore keys ssh-keys/<nodeId>; nodes.yaml stores only authMethod and credentialRef. Password and private-key SSH authentication are mutually exclusive.

nodes.label: Agent kernels List
nodes.rationale: Agent configs use a non-empty, duplicate-free kernels list of sing-box, Xray, and/or V2Ray entries, each with an optional config path; a node may use kernels: [] draft form until its first successful deploy.

nodes.label: Vercel Git Integration
nodes.rationale: Production runs on Vercel; Vercel Git Integration builds and publishes production when main is pushed, replacing the old SSH upload, symlink switch, systemd restart, and scheduled SSH health check flow.

nodes.label: scripts/ensure-mihomo-binary.mjs
nodes.rationale: Vercel Linux builds run scripts/ensure-mihomo-binary.mjs writing frontend/bin/mihomo; Next outputFileTracingIncludes bundles it into server functions so mihomo is available in the read-only function bundle.

nodes.label: frontend/next.config.js standalone output
nodes.rationale: frontend/next.config.js enables output: 'standalone' only outside Vercel; Vercel uses its native Next.js builder output.

nodes.label: Atomic systemd Unit Replacement
nodes.rationale: Remote Agent config and systemd unit replacement use checked same-directory temporary files and atomic moves; sudo passwords travel through SSH stdin to avoid credential exposure.

nodes.label: Child Deployment Kernel Detection
nodes.rationale: Child deployment detects every supported kernel, lets operators choose the monitored set, installs selected missing kernels, then deploys one Agent config; new nodes persist as empty-kernel drafts and successful/partial deploys atomically commit only monitored kernels.

nodes.label: Removed deploy.yml Workflow
nodes.rationale: The old SSH deploy.yml and scheduled SSH health-check.yml workflows were removed because production deploys now use Vercel Git Integration; GitHub Actions is a CI gate only and no longer uses SSH, tarball upload, symlink switching, systemd restart, or Vercel CLI.

nodes.label: NodeManager Config Init Before Logger
nodes.rationale: NodeManager now initializes MioBridge/config before importing the shared logger; this avoids Bun leaving config in a TDZ while preserving the configured logging level and directory.

nodes.label: FileStateStore Base Dir Normalization
nodes.rationale: FileStateStore normalizes its configured base directory before path-containment checks, so trailing separators no longer reject valid keys.

nodes.label: Atomic Rename for Agent systemd Deploy
nodes.rationale: Agent config and systemd unit deployment uses checked, same-directory temporary files plus atomic rename; sudo passwords travel over SSH stdin, node YAML strings use JSON-compatible quoting, and proxy logs omit credentials and complete URLs.
nodes.norm_label: atomic rename for agent systemd deploy

nodes.label: buildAgentOnRemote
nodes.rationale: Agent deploy no longer requires the ~100MB compiled binary in the control plane bundle; when agent/miobridge-agent is missing locally (Vercel), the target host downloads the repo tarball pinned to the control plane commit and compiles the agent with its own Bun. Self-hosted control planes with the binary still SFTP-upload.

nodes.label: Public HMAC Agent Endpoints
nodes.rationale: Normal Agent status/health/URL checks use public HMAC endpoints only; SSH fallback is reserved for deployment and diagnostics so routine monitoring does not require SSH access.

nodes.label: StateStore Registry and Progress Persistence
nodes.rationale: Node registry and deploy progress no longer vanish between Vercel function instances because nodes.yaml IO and deployProgressStore go through the StateStore abstraction (file backend by default, Redis REST when configured); without Redis env vars on Vercel the old ephemeral behavior remains with a startup warning.

nodes.label: Mio Garden UI
nodes.rationale: Make Mio Garden the primary web control surface using shadcn/ui primitives, Iconify icons, Botanical Garden tokens, and focused motion interactions; flat Material 3 inspired panels with no visible borders.

nodes.label: Config Management Goal
nodes.rationale: Edit runtime config without SSH: read/write ~/.config/miobridge/config.yaml through service APIs, validate YAML and supported fields before write, backup before every write with rollback, hot-reload safe fields, and clearly mark fields requiring service restart.

nodes.label: Subscription Sources Goal
nodes.rationale: Stop relying only on local sing-box config names; support sing-box config, remote URL, manual text, and uploaded node files, test each source before saving, track status/node count/latency/failure reason, dedupe nodes with rename/filter rules.

nodes.label: Output Customization Goal
nodes.rationale: Let users shape generated Clash output: built-in templates, proxy group and rule editing, validate generated clash.yaml with mihomo, export YAML/install URL/QR code.

nodes.label: Observability Goal
nodes.rationale: Diagnose from the web UI before SSH: search/filter logs by level/time/keyword, record update history/duration/node count/errors, add /api/metrics or equivalent structured metrics, dashboard alerts and optional webhook notifications.

nodes.label: API and Quality Goal
nodes.rationale: Make the service easier to integrate and safer to change: publish OpenAPI docs for stable endpoints, standardize API errors and request validation, add unit/integration/E2E coverage for key flows, include tests in CI once stable enough to gate PRs.

nodes.label: v1.0 Distributed Node Control Plane
nodes.rationale: Shipped baseline: main node, Agent, HMAC, NodeManager, remote deployment, adapters; main node generates raw.txt/subscription.txt/clash.yaml, child nodes run Agent/kernel and expose source URLs, remote checks use public Agent HTTP plus HMAC with SSH for deploy/diagnosis.

nodes.rationale: Framework-independent backend logic lives in packages/core and is exposed through the MioBridgeCore facade so the Next.js frontend and the standalone Linux CLI can compose the same public core API without duplication.

nodes.label: Public Compatibility URLs
nodes.rationale: Compatibility paths /subscription.txt, /clash.yaml, /raw.txt, /health are served by Next rewrites to internal API routes so public URLs stay stable while implementation lives inside API routes.

nodes.label: HMAC Auth Middleware
nodes.rationale: Inter-node communication is secured with HMAC-signed HTTP requests plus replay protection because the main node talks to remote Agents over public HTTP rather than SSH.

nodes.label: mihomo Conversion Kernel
nodes.rationale: mihomo is invoked as a local command-line conversion kernel to replace the external subconverter dependency.

nodes.label: R7 Permission-Friendly Boundaries
nodes.rationale: Pages remain separate because future multi-user permissions will map to page-level capabilities. Pages are permission boundaries; the workflow is cross-page.

nodes.label: Signal Room Visual Language
nodes.rationale: Signal Room treats subscription conversion as a signal operation: a control room for routes, files, agents, and outputs with one emerald accent, graphite surfaces, and double-bezel panels. It retires the decorative Botanical Garden identity in favor of an operational aesthetic.

nodes.label: Vercel Git Integration Deploy
nodes.rationale: Production deploys are handled by Vercel Git Integration rather than GitHub Actions, so no SSH deployment secrets or Vercel CLI token are needed in CI. The old SSH/systemd deploy.yml and health-check.yml workflows were removed.

nodes.label: Failure Source Panel
nodes.rationale: Summarizes each pipeline stage (kernel/agent/subscription/mihomo/file-write) with a status badge (正常/警告/超时/通过) so users can locate the failing stage before reading raw logs.

nodes.label: Evidence Log Panel (real-time stream)
nodes.rationale: Streams production.log in real time and cross-references the active failure with a 定位/节点/影响 triad so the user can confirm root cause in the same view.

nodes.label: Real-time Log Stream Pattern
nodes.rationale: Tail production.log with timestamps and severity tags (INFO/WARN) so operators see causality between the WARN timeout and the subsequent INFO fallback that produced clash.yaml.

nodes.label: MioBridge Logs/Diagnostics Page
nodes.rationale: Failure-source diagnostics workflow: first locate the problem by failure source, then confirm details with raw logs. The page surfaces categorized failure sources on the left and live evidence logs on the right so operators can move from category to concrete log line in one view.

nodes.label: Failure Sources Panel
nodes.rationale: Each failure source is rendered as a row with a status pill: 正常 (Normal), 警告 (Warning), 超时 (Timeout), 通过 (Passed). This categorizes the diagnostic state per subsystem so the operator can see at a glance which stage is unhealthy.

nodes.label: Evidence Log Panel
nodes.rationale: Right pane shows a live-stream (实时流) raw log from production.log, paired with a structured triage header: 定位 (Location) -> 节点 (Node) -> 影响 (Impact). This binds the abstract failure category to a concrete node and to a downstream artifact (clash.yaml).

nodes.label: View Recommended Actions Button
nodes.rationale: The failure-sources pane ends with a single primary CTA. Design intent: collapse the categorized failures into a single next action so the operator does not have to read every row to know what to do.

nodes.label: Live Tail Indicator
nodes.rationale: A persistent 'live tail' badge in the sidebar signals the page is streaming. The status header on the right shows '订阅源超时已归类' (subscription timeout categorized) - the page does real-time classification, not just log display.
